text: Zara has a real flair for the theatric.  Her method of greeting customers at her shop is showy but effective.
speaker: erasmus

text: It's a pity she has no sense of humor.
speaker: erasmus

text: Just because she never laughs at your jokes doesn't mean that she lacks a sense of humor.
speaker: fenrus

text: It just means that she has good taste.
speaker: fenrus

text:
Baba Yaga is good at curses and shape-
changing spells.  She has a nasty temper, so it's best to stay on her good side.
speaker: erasmus

text: You have to watch her.  She cheats at cards.
speaker: erasmus

text: So do you!
speaker: fenrus

text: She started it!  I just wanted to give her a taste of her own medicine.
speaker: erasmus

text: It's a shame she still beats you!
speaker: fenrus

text: The curse Baba Yaga placed upon the Baron von Spielburg was a fine example of creative cursing.
speaker: erasmus

text: "Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:   What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
speaker: erasmus

text: Of course, the problem is that for every curse there is an equal and opposite countercurse.
speaker: erasmus

text: A countercurse is the cure for a curse.  It usually works against the one who cast the curse in the first place.
speaker: erasmus

text: The countercurse for the curse on the Baron goes:
speaker: erasmus

text: "Come a hero from the East..."
speaker: erasmus

text: "Free the man from in the beast..."
speaker: erasmus

text: "Bring the child from out the band..."
speaker: erasmus

text: "Drive the Curser from the land."
speaker: erasmus

text: So, to break the curse, a hero must get rid of Baba Yaga, in addition to all the other things.  It does not reflect well on Baba Yaga.
speaker: erasmus

text: Nothing reflects well upon that Ogress.  She has a face that would break all but a Magic Mirror.
speaker: fenrus

text: What am I, chopped liver?  Am I invisible or something that you talk about me behind my front?
speaker: magic-mirror

text: It's hard to take you seriously when you wear that silly hat!
speaker: fenrus

text: Henry is a hermit who lives by the Flying Falls to the south. Great sense of humor.
speaker: erasmus

text: That means he actually laughs at Erasmus' jokes.
speaker: fenrus

text: I think he borrowed something the last time I saw him, but I can't remember exactly what it was.
speaker: erasmus

text: Oh, well, Henry is a great one to talk to.
speaker: erasmus

text: But 'e 'ardly ever 'appens to 'ave much to say.
speaker: fenrus

text: You need to complete your quest here before you can become initiated as a Wizard.
speaker: erasmus

text: There are many types of magic mirrors.  One of the more common types is the one that has a strange face popping up every so often.
speaker: erasmus

text: Reminds me of the one in the master bedroom.
speaker: fenrus

text: That's not a magic mirror.
speaker: erasmus

text: No, but it does show a strange face.  Have you looked in it lately?
speaker: fenrus

text: At any rate, I used to have a nice magic hand mirror, but I misplaced it somewhere.  It could be used to reflect a spell back upon the caster.
speaker: erasmus

text: So, if you happen upon a small golden magic mirror with a dragon on the handle, you'll know it's mine.
speaker: erasmus

text: It was the flagon with the dragon.  The mirror had a leer.
speaker: fenrus

text: Oh.  That's right!
speaker: erasmus

text: Well, if you find a magic mirror with a face on it, you'll know it reflects spells.
speaker: erasmus

text: Basically, anyone who uses magic is a magic user;   anyone who casts spells is a spellcaster.
speaker: erasmus

text: However, in order to be a wizard, you need to have undergone initiation into the "Wizard's Institute of Technocery" in the South.
speaker: erasmus

text: Magicians and sorcerers are wizards who specialize.
speaker: erasmus

text:
For myself, I prefer to master all aspects of Magic unlike the narrow-
minded specialists.
speaker: erasmus

text: You wouldn't want to lose your amateur standing, after all.
speaker: fenrus

text: I prefer to think of myself as a dilettante.
speaker: erasmus

text: Does that mean you tease pickles?
speaker: fenrus

text: He laughs too much when he casts his spells.
speaker: erasmus

text: That's because his jokes are funnier than yours.
speaker: fenrus

text: Isn't that like the vampire who likes to take necks out for a good time?
speaker: fenrus

text: No, no!  That's a "neck romancer"!
speaker: erasmus

text: A necromancer is a magic user who has a rather unsavory relationship with corpses.
speaker: erasmus

text: Does that mean a necromancer has ghoul friends?
speaker: fenrus

text: It means, my dear Fenrus, that a necromancer is someone who deals with the dead.
speaker: erasmus

text: Canasta with cadavers?
speaker: fenrus

text: Spades with shades?
speaker: erasmus

text: "If Magic be the lute of life, play on!"
speaker: erasmus

text: The quote was "Music", not "Magic"!  And it was "food", not "lute"!
speaker: fenrus

text: How about:  "Magic makes the world go 'round"?
speaker: erasmus

text: "MONEY makes the world go 'round"!
speaker: fenrus

text: Very well, then.  To put it in my own words:  "Magic is the essence and the soul of life, and the Wizard is Her poet".
speaker: erasmus

text: Actually, it was Merlin who said that first, but he used the term "Magician", not "Wizard".
speaker: fenrus

text: Fenrus, there are times when you'd look better as a newt!
speaker: erasmus

text: Why is that?
speaker: fenrus

text: Because newts can't talk!
speaker: erasmus

text: Neither can most rats.
speaker: fenrus

text: Just a little something to challenge the mind and the magic.  I like to play it every now and then, but Fenrus isn't much of a challenge anymore.
speaker: erasmus

text: That's because I always win.
speaker: fenrus

text: I still think you're cheating, somehow!
speaker: erasmus

text: Anyway, the game involves casting four spells:   "Flame Dart", "Open", "Fetch", and "Trigger".
speaker: erasmus

text: And to make it worth your while, if you beat me, I'll teach you a spell called "Dazzle".
speaker: erasmus

text: You'll have to climb down to do that.
speaker: narrator

text: A river plunges for more than a hundred feet down the face of a cliff.  A doorway has been built into the side of the cliff.
speaker: narrator

text: The water seems to be trying to fly as it leaps from the mountain above.
speaker: narrator

text: You see slick, vertical cliff walls.
speaker: narrator

text: The water is white as it splashes against the rocks with great force and purpose and disappears into a narrow canyon to the south.
speaker: narrator

text: The grass is green with the freshness of new spring.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a narrow ledge in front of the wide door.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see a ladder here.
speaker: narrator

text: The door is built into the face of the mountainside.  You suspect that the person who lives behind it treasures privacy.
speaker: narrator

text: It's a large door.  It must open pretty wide.
speaker: narrator

text: you don't have a flask
speaker: narrator

text: The water comes from melting snow.  This early in the season, it's much too cold to swim.
speaker: narrator

text: The door is securely locked.
speaker: narrator

text: The door is so securely locked that it defies your abilities to unlock it.
speaker: narrator

text: It's a little hard to knock on the door from where you're standing.
speaker: narrator

text: You're in front of the door already.  You don't need to climb any farther.
speaker: narrator

text: As you prepare your spell...
speaker: narrator

text: RAP!

text: "All right, already!"
speaker: hermit

text: "Is someone there?"

text: "You again?  Either come up, or go away.   I really doesn't cares which."
speaker: hermit

text: "Oh, 'ello.  Come right up."
speaker: hermit

text: "Just climb the ladder."
speaker: hermit

text: You take a drink of water from the icy mountain river.  It refreshes you.
speaker: narrator

text: You take another drink of water.  You were thirsty!
speaker: narrator

text: BOY!  You must have really been thirsty!
speaker: narrator

text: You fill an empty flask with crystal-clear water from the waterfall.
speaker: narrator

text: Flat...you feel very, very flat.
speaker: narrator

text: And dead...you feel dead, too.  In your weakened condition, you succumbed to a mild-mannered hermit's propensity for Tarzan imitations.  Back up and play it again, Sam.
speaker: narrator

text: That hermit seems to know his "Trigger" spells pretty well.  He sure pulled the trigger on you (not to mention the plug) by teleporting you to the top of the falls without a barrel.
speaker: narrator

text: You knock three times.
speaker: narrator

text: "Just a minute!  Just a minute!"
speaker: hermit

text: You've already made a mess of that flower.
speaker: narrator

text: There's not much point in that, now that you've already done what you came here to do.

text: Not now!

text: To the north and west are steep cliffs.   There are some unusual plants growing out of the side of the cliffs.
speaker: narrator

text: To the south and east is the forest from which you came.
speaker: narrator

text: To the south and east is the forest from which you came. To the north and west are steep cliffs. There are some unusual plants growing out of the side of the cliffs.

text: The cliff walls are very steep, and they look difficult to climb.
speaker: narrator

speaker: narrator

text: The leaves look tough and fibrous.
speaker: narrator

text: If you had a sword, you might be able to do that.

text: Wait a second!

speaker: narrator

text: You might have tried to cast a spell at the seed, but it's too late now.

text: You've never seen anything quite like them. They're pretty, in a grotesque way.
